intro: Moving from Java to Ruby trades rigid, compiler-enforced architectural structures for an elegant, highly expressive language that prioritizes developer happiness and rapid prototyping. Ruby makes everything an object and exposes metadata dynamically, enabling powerful metaprogramming patterns that drastically reduce boilerplate code. This transition is common when moving from legacy enterprise backends to agile, convention-over-configuration web frameworks like Ruby on Rails.

diffs.from: Strictly nominal, manifest, and validated by static compiler compilation blocks.
diffs.to: Completely dynamic, relying entirely on duck typing where object behaviors matter more than specific inheritance lineages.

diffs.from: Verbose, class-bound code blocks relying on semi-colons, curly braces, and mandatory variable declarations.
diffs.to: Highly concise, human-readable expression syntax that omits parenthesis, semicolons, and return statements by default.

diffs.from: Managed via complex, highly tunable JVM background collectors optimizing long-lived heap object references.
diffs.to: Managed by the YARV runtime utilizing a generational mark-and-sweep garbage collector built for quick application turns.

diffs.from: Native OS multi-threading concurrent task pipelines and modern high-throughput virtual threads.
diffs.to: Constrained by a Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) in YARV, relying on Fibers, Ractors, or multi-process architectures for concurrency.

diffs.from: Monolithic, enterprise-grade class libraries designed to ensure backward compatibility across decades.
diffs.to: Flexible, developer-focused core library specializing in advanced string processing, collection manipulation, and system scripting.

examples.title: Collection Transformation via Blocks
examples.description: Refactoring a Java stream mapping pipeline into an elegant Ruby block transformation loop.
examples.toCode:
class Transform
  def upper(words)
    words.map(&:upcase)
  end
end

examples.title: Dynamic Method Execution
examples.description: Contrasting verbose Java reflection boilerplate with Ruby's clean dynamic method invocation utilities.
examples.toCode:
class Dispatch
  def run(obj, method_name)
    obj.send(method_name)
  end
end

tips.title: Trust Duck Typing Behavior
tips.body: Stop checking object types before invoking actions; verify capability boundaries instead by utilizing responses to respond_to? calls.

tips.title: Omit Optional Syntactic Elements
tips.body: Clean up your source code appearance by removing unnecessary return statements and method-call parentheses wherever readability allows.

tips.title: Leverage Blocks and Yields
tips.body: Master passing anonymous code snippets directly into methods using yield blocks to handle resource wraps and structural iterations cleanly.

tips.title: Embrace Convention Over Configuration
tips.body: Avoid configuring systems via XML or annotation frameworks. Let Ruby's standard naming patterns infer class connections automatically.
